headline: Truist Cuts PT on Amcor plc (AMCR) to $50 Amid Higher Energy and Freight Costs
summary: Amcor plc (NYSE:AMCR) is one of the best long-term ASX stocks to buy right now. As of April 15, 2026, Amcor plc (NYSE:AMCR) continues to retain support from Truist, even though the firm lowered its price target, signaling a more cautious near-term stance on costs rather than a break in the broader earnings story. Truist […]

headline: TFC Q1 Deep Dive: Fee Income and Disciplined Growth Shape Outlook Amid Rate Pressures
summary: Financial services company Truist Financial (NYSE:TFC) met Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 5% year on year to $5.20 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.09 per share was 9% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Truist Maintains Buy Rating on ServiceNow (NOW)
summary: ServiceNow, Inc. (NYSE:NOW) is one of the 10 Best Stocks to Buy for the Next Decade. On April 15, 2026, Truist lowered its price target on ServiceNow, Inc. (NYSE:NOW) to $125 from $175 and maintained a Buy rating ahead of quarterly results. The firm said it expects strong performance with potential upside to consensus estimates, […]

headline: How The CAVA Group (CAVA) Growth Narrative Is Evolving With New Guidance And Valuation Concerns
summary: CAVA Group’s blended fair value estimate now sits at about US$85.83 per share, compared with a prior figure of US$84, a modest 2.2% adjustment that has caught investors’ attention. This move comes as Street analysts react to recent earnings and 2026 guidance, weighing strong same store sales and unit economics against questions about how much of the upside case is already reflected in the share price. Read on to see what is driving these shifts and how you can follow the evolving analyst...
